subject: Sociology
title: Differential Association Theory
description: A quick, practical guide to Differential Association Theory.

hook.text: Kids who hang out with delinquents become delinquent. Is it peer pressure or something more systematic?

buildup.text: Edwin Sutherland argued that criminal behavior is learned through intimate personal groups, not born within.

discovery.text: People learn not just techniques but also attitudes: when crime is acceptable, justified, and expected.

twist.text: This applies to white-collar crime too. Corporate cultures teach employees that fraud is just good business.

climax.text: Crime isn't about bad people. It's about social environments that teach criminal behavior as normal.

quiz.question: What does differential association theory argue?
options: Criminal behavior is learned through social groups
